title: ageratina aegirophylla
intro: Ageratina aegirophylla, also known as White Mint, is a plant species in the mint family native to Mexico and Central America. Its leaves and stems possess medicinal properties, particularly in their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial abilities. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Ageratina aegirophylla can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil for topical use, or dried for inclusion in herbal blends.
